subject: Anthropology
title: Exogamy and Endogamy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Exogamy and Endogamy.

hook.text: You must marry outside your clan. Or you must marry within your caste. Marriage rules dictate who is off-limits.

buildup.text: Exogamy requires marrying outside your group. Endogamy requires marrying within it. Both shape social boundaries.

discovery.text: Exogamy builds alliances between groups by forcing intermarriage. Endogamy preserves group identity and resources.

twist.text: Most societies use both. You may be exogamous with your clan but endogamous within your class, race, or religion.

climax.text: Marriage rules are boundary-making tools. They decide who belongs, who connects, and who stays separate.

punchline.text: Who you can't marry matters as much as who you can.
